2026-04-21T05:26:57.2865654Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2866169Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2866575Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2866925Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2867219Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2867449Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2867606Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2867804Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2867983Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2868214Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T05:26:57.2868692Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:26:57.2868937Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABgAAAAYCAYAAADgdz34AAAABmJLR0QA/wD/AP+gvaeTAAABxElEQVRIibWVvW4TURCFv0vlNEDcIHAkKAERJOIK...

Docs: README missing coverage of GetUsage() and ContentUnderstandingClientSettings (1.1.0)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58166 (commit `10f785b`) by `@yungshinlintw`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58166 added two new public APIs in version 1.1.0:\\n\\n1. **`AnalyzeUsageDetails` class + `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` extension method** — surfaces billing and token-consumption details (document page counts, contextualization tokens, per-model LLM/embedding token breakdown) from a completed analyze operation.\\n2. **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` class** — supports creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration`, enabling configuration-file-based credential resolution and ASP.NET Core dependency injection registration.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples section** lists samples but does not mention the new `GetUsage()` / usage/billing tracking capability (covered in `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` but not surfaced in the README).\\n- The README **Key concepts** and **Authenticate the client** sections do not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` or IConfiguration-based setup.\\n- The README **Examples section** does not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`/configuration-based client construction.\\n- There is no standalone sample file (`.md` / `.cs`) for `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` — no equivalent of the other `SampleXX_*.md` files exists for this feature.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n**1a. Add `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` to the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; section**\\n\\nAfter the existing `#### Using API key` subsection, add a new subsection:\\n\\n````markdown\\n#### Using IConfiguration (ASP.NET Core / dependency injection)\\n\\n`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` supports loading client configuration — endpoint and credentials — from `IConfiguration`, which is the recommended pattern for ASP.NET Core applications.\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ContentUnderstandingCreateClientFromSettings\\n// In your appsettings.json:\\n// {\\n//   \\u0026#34;ContentUnderstanding\\u0026#34;: {\\n//     \\u0026#34;Endpoint\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;https://.services.ai.azure.com/\\u0026#34;\\n//   }\\n// }\\nIConfiguration configuration = new ConfigurationBuilder()\\n    .AddJsonFile(\\u0026#34;appsettings.json\\u0026#34;)\\n    .Build();\\n\\nvar settings = new ContentUnderstandingClientSettings();\\nsettings.Bind(configuration.GetSection(\\u0026#34;ContentUnderstanding\\u0026#34;));\\nvar client = new ContentUnderstandingClient(settings);\\n```\\n````\\n\\n**1b. Add a bullet for usage/billing tracking in the Examples section**\\n\\nInside the `## Examples` → bullet list, append:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* **Usage and billing details** - Retrieve token consumption, document page counts, and per-model LLM/embedding usage after a completed analyze operation using `GetUsage()`\\n* **Configuration-based client creation** - Create a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration` using `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` for ASP.NET Core dependency injection scenarios\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update the \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** to reference the new sample:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* [Sample 16: Get usage/billing details][sample16] — Retrieve `AnalyzeUsageDetails` from a completed analyze operation\\n```\\n\\nAdd the corresponding footnote at the bottom of the README where the other `[sampleXX]` links are defined:\\n\\n```markdown\\n[sample16]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/samples/Sample16_GetUsage.md\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet A: ContentUnderstandingClientSettings (new test file)\\n\\nCreate a new test sample file at:  \\n`sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/tests/samples/Sample16_GetUsage.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\n#nullable enable\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure;\\nusing Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding;\\nusing Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.Tests;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ContentUnderstandingSamples\\n    {\\n        [RecordedTest]\\n        public async Task GetUsageDetailsAsync()\\n        {\\n            string endpoint = TestEnvironment.Endpoint;\\n            var options = InstrumentClientOptions(new ContentUnderstandingClientOptions());\\n            var client = InstrumentClient(new ContentUnderstandingClient(new Uri(endpoint), TestEnvironment.Credential, options));\\n\\n            Uri invoiceUrl = new Uri(\\u0026#34;https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure-Samples/azure-ai-content-understanding-dotnet/main/ContentUnderstanding.Common/data/invoice.pdf\\u0026#34;);\\n            Operation operation = await client.AnalyzeAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;prebuilt-invoice\\u0026#34;,\\n                inputs: new[] { new AnalysisInput { Uri = invoiceUrl } });\\n\\n            #region Snippet:ContentUnderstandingGetUsage\\n            // Get usage details (token consumption, page counts) from the completed operation.\\n            AnalyzeUsageDetails? usage = operation.GetUsage();\\n            if (usage != null)\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Document pages (standard): {usage.DocumentPagesStandard}\\u0026#34;);\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Contextualization tokens: {usage.ContextualizationTokens}\\u0026#34;);\\n                foreach (var kvp in usage.Tokens)\\n                {\\n                    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  {kvp.Key}: {kvp.Value}\\u0026#34;);\\n                }\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            if (Mode != RecordedTestMode.Playback)\\n            {\\n                Assert.IsNotNull(usage, \\u0026#34;Usage details should be available after a completed analysis\\u0026#34;);\\n                Assert.IsNotNull(usage!.Tokens, \\u0026#34;Tokens dictionary should not be null\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet name:** `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage`\\n\\n**README placeholder location:** In the new `Sample16_GetUsage.md` (see Step 3 below), place it after the introductory prose.\\n\\n#### Snippet B: ContentUnderstandingClientSettings\\n\\nAdd a `#region Snippet:ContentUnderstandingCreateClientFromSettings` / `#endregion` block to the same `Sample16_GetUsage.cs` file (or a new `Sample17_CreateClientFromSettings.cs`), showing how to instantiate a client from `IConfiguration`. Then add a matching placeholder in the README as described in Step 1a above.\\n\\n### Step 3: Create new sample markdown file\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/samples/Sample16_GetUsage.md`  \\n**Action:** Create\\n\\n````markdown\\n# Sample 16: Get usage and billing details\\n\\nAfter an analyze operation completes, you can retrieve usage details that describe the resources consumed — including document page counts, contextualization tokens, and per-model LLM/embedding token breakdown.\\n\\n## Get usage details\\n\\nCall `GetUsage()` on the completed `Operation` to obtain an `AnalyzeUsageDetails` instance:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ContentUnderstandingGetUsage\\n// Get usage details (token consumption, page counts) from the completed operation.\\nAnalyzeUsageDetails? usage = operation.GetUsage();\\nif (usage != null)\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Document pages (standard): {usage.DocumentPagesStandard}\\u0026#34;);\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Contextualization tokens: {usage.ContextualizationTokens}\\u0026#34;);\\n    foreach (var kvp in usage.Tokens)\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  {kvp.Key}: {kvp.Value}\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe `AnalyzeUsageDetails` object exposes:\\n\\n| Property | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `DocumentPagesMinimal` | Pages processed at minimal level |\\n| `DocumentPagesBasic` | Pages processed at basic level |\\n| `DocumentPagesStandard` | Pages processed at standard level |\\n| `AudioHours` | Hours of audio processed |\\n| `VideoHours` | Hours of video processed |\\n| `ContextualizationTokens` | Tokens used for context preparation, confidence scoring, and output formatting |\\n| `Tokens` | Per-model LLM/embedding token counts (e.g., `\\u0026#34;gpt-4.1-input\\u0026#34;`, `\\u0026#34;gpt-4.1-output\\u0026#34;`) |\\n````\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — confirm README and sample markdown files are updated correctly (check git diff)\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 contentunderstanding` — no public API changes are expected; verify no diff\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; Docs: README missing coverage of GetUsage() and ContentUnderstandingClientSettings (1.1.0)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58166 (commit `10f785b`) by `@yungshinlintw`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58166 added two new public APIs in version 1.1.0:\\n\\n1. **`AnalyzeUsageDetails` class + `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` extension method** — surfaces billing and token-consumption details (document page counts, contextualization tokens, per-model LLM/embedding token breakdown) from a completed analyze operation.\\n2. **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` class** — supports creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration`, enabling configuration-file-based credential resolution and ASP.NET Core dependency injection registration.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples section** lists samples but does not mention the new `GetUsage()` / usage/billing tracking capability (covered in `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` but not surfaced in the README).\\n- The README **Key concepts** and **Authenticate the client** sections do not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` or IConfiguration-based setup.\\n- The README **Examples section** does not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`/configuration-based client construction.\\n- There is no standalone sample file (`.md` / `.cs`) for `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` — no equivalent of the other `SampleXX_*.md` files exists for this feature.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n**1a. usage = operation.GetUsage();\\n            if (usage != null)\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Document pages (standard): {usage.DocumentPagesStandard}\\u0026#34;);\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Contextualization tokens: {usage.ContextualizationTokens}\\u0026#34;);\\n                foreach (var kvp in usage.Tokens)\\n                {\\n                    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;  {kvp.Key}: {kvp.Value}\\u0026#34;);\\n                }\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            if (Mode != RecordedTestMode.Playback)\\n            {\\n                Assert.IsNotNull(usage, \\u0026#34;Usage details should be available after a completed analysis\\u0026#34;);\\n                Assert.IsNotNull(usage!.Tokens, \\u0026#34;Tokens dictionary should not be null\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet name:** `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage`\\n\\n**README placeholder location:** In the new `Sample16_GetUsage.md` (see Step 3 below), place it after the introductory prose.\\n\\n#### Snippet B: ContentUnderstandingClientSettings\\n\\nAdd a `#region Snippet:ContentUnderstandingCreateClientFromSettings` / `#endregion` block to the same `Sample16_GetUsage.cs` file (or a new `Sample17_CreateClientFromSettings.cs`), showing how to instantiate a client from `IConfiguration`.
